Explanation: This report presents a comprehensive HPLC data analysis addressing system stability, gradient alignment, and product purity. Strengths include clear problem articulation, robust methodology employing Asymmetric Least Squares baseline correction, and meaningful quantitative findings (479 high-risk fractions identified). The writing is generally professional with well-structured sections. However, some methodology descriptions lack precision in the body text due to apparent truncation. The large multipart visualization effectively displays corrected UV signals, gradient profiles, and pressure traces across runs and columns. Limitations include modest novelty (standard HPLC analysis techniques) and incomplete explanation of certain technical choices. The critical finding—that column chemistry significantly impacts purity ratios—is valuable but could benefit from deeper mechanistic investigation. Code inclusion enhances reproducibility. The 75% metadata gap and aggregation strategy based on run numbers rather than samples somewhat limits conclusions, though this constraint is acknowledged. Mathematical rigor is adequate with appropriate statistical thresholds (mean ±3σ), though no confidence intervals or uncertainty analysis are provided.
